reviewer_concern: Chemical Engineering Science reviewers may ask whether Ib is supported by pressure-drop or CFD data.
primary_artifacts: Fig. 6; Fig. S3; Fig. S22; Fig. S23; Tables S6-S7, S10-S11, S15
closure_evidence: Ib is kept pressure-free; Ergun, scalar voxel-network and cropped-domain OpenFOAM checks are reported as bounded pressure-flow evidence.
boundary_to_preserve: The manuscript states that Ib is a pressure-free structural pre-screening index and that the Ergun result is only a proxy.

reviewer_concern: A reviewer could reject any claim of a universal transition because connectivity loss is not measurable in the current cases.
closure_evidence: Current cases show migration, weak breakthrough and local blockage with zero measurable connectivity loss; transition language is explicitly excluded.
boundary_to_preserve: The manuscript frames the result as pre-clogging migration/filtering and states that identifying a bed-scale clogging threshold requires higher loading, localized release, or coupled flow.

reviewer_concern: The final BTC values are not monotonic with injected debris count, so reviewers may challenge trend claims.
primary_artifacts: Fig. 7; Fig. 9; Table S1; Table S14
closure_evidence: The loading scan is used as bounded single-seed response evidence; repeat-seed evidence is confined to the near-threshold fixed-bed window.
boundary_to_preserve: The manuscript says the loading data are bounded loading responses rather than a monotonic breakthrough law.

reviewer_concern: The localized-release evidence now covers the planned target-time rows, but the case family is still too sparse to fit a general source-position or source-strength law.
primary_artifacts: Fig. 4; Fig. 5; Fig. S8-S10; Fig. S19; Tables S1, S12-S13
closure_evidence: All three localized-release production rows reached target time and are interpreted as mechanism evidence rather than a source-position law.
boundary_to_preserve: 3/3 localized-release production jobs reached target physical time; the manuscript treats them as mechanism evidence rather than a fitted source-location law.

reviewer_concern: The study has no physical CT/PIV experiment, so imaging terminology must be precise.
primary_artifacts: Fig. 1; Fig. 2; Fig. S1; Tables S2-S4
closure_evidence: The manuscript and figure labels use DEM-derived voxel reconstruction or pore field wording, not experimental CT wording.
boundary_to_preserve: The manuscript uses DEM-derived pore reconstruction and explicitly rejects experimental computed-tomography interpretation.

reviewer_concern: Reviewers may ask whether drag forcing is physically realistic inside a tortuous packed bed.
primary_artifacts: Fig. 3; Fig. S16; Fig. S23; Fig. S24; Fig. S26; Tables S11-S12, S18
closure_evidence: Drag-to-weight trends are presented as one-way forcing results; Supplementary Table S18 documents debris diameter, nominal particle Reynolds number and recomputed Stokes drag-to-weight range; OpenFOAM checks are cropped-domain pressure-flow checks, not two-way DEM-CFD.
boundary_to_preserve: The manuscript states that the forcing is one-way Stokes drag, reports a dimensional drag-scaling audit, and does not solve the local gas field.

reviewer_concern: If the skeleton moves, transport differences could reflect bed rearrangement rather than debris migration.
closure_evidence: Transport cases use a fixed compacted skeleton so debris migration is not confounded by large-pebble rearrangement.
boundary_to_preserve: Formal transport cases use a fixed compacted skeleton so the response is attributed to debris parameters.

reviewer_concern: Figures with text-heavy panels, black slices, or unclear provenance weaken a mechanism-first journal submission.
primary_artifacts: Figure provenance manifest; Fig. 1; Fig. 6; Fig. 8; Fig. 11
closure_evidence: Figure provenance and revised captions keep panels data-linked and boundary-labeled without large explanatory text blocks.
boundary_to_preserve: A figure provenance manifest records source scripts, exports, and claim boundaries for each figure.

reviewer_concern: The target journal title should be concise and should not promise unavailable validation or critical transition evidence.
closure_evidence: The title and framing avoid experiment, CT and critical-transition claims and present a bounded DEM mechanism study.
boundary_to_preserve: The current title is Chemical Engineering Science-oriented and DEM-bounded: Separating breakthrough from pore-network clogging indicators in gas-driven fine-debris transport through Li4SiO4 pebble beds.

reviewer_concern: Reviewers may ask whether the 3D resistance-amplification result is a calibrated pore-flow or pressure-drop calculation.
primary_artifacts: Fig. S22; Fig. S23; Fig. S24; Fig. S26; Tables S10-S11, S15
closure_evidence: Reduced hydraulic sensitivity is labeled as scalar voxel-network evidence; OpenFOAM pressure evidence is separated as cropped-domain numerical checking.
boundary_to_preserve: The manuscript and figure provenance identify Fig. S22/Table S15 as a scalar voxel-network sensitivity test, not OpenFOAM CFD, LBM, measured pressure or pressure-calibrated Ib.

reviewer_concern: Journal submission requires data availability, funding, competing-interest, and acknowledgement statements.
closure_evidence: The submission gate keeps administrative confirmation separate from scientific evidence readiness.
